title: Business Development Manager, Spanish Speaking - TikTok LIVE - Madrid (12-Month Fixed-term Contract))
description:
Responsibilities
The TikTok LIVE Operations team is seeking a dynamic, high-performing Business Development Manager with a proven ability to identify and acquire emerging agencies and entrepreneurs across Spain.

The ideal candidate excels within a fast-paced work environment, has a strong network within the entrepreneurial ecosystem, and demonstrates the ability to spot high-potential partners who can succeed in the TikTok LIVE ecosystem. This role focuses on expanding our network of agency partners, driving growth through strategic acquisitions, and fostering entrepreneurial success. The candidate should be comfortable with a non-standard job that involves everything from hosting events to casual coffee chats, all aimed at developing the market and building meaningful connections.

Key Responsibilities:
- Identify and strategically recruit emerging agencies, entrepreneurs, and organizations in Spain to join the TikTok LIVE ecosystem.
- Build and leverage a strong network of entrepreneurs to spot potential partners with high growth potential.
- Develop and execute strategies for acquiring and nurturing partnerships with agencies that align with TikTok LIVE’s goals.
- Monitor and analyze the performance of acquired partners, ensuring alignment with TikTok’s objectives.
- Provide insights and feedback to optimize partnership acquisition strategies and drive business growth.
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to align on operational goals and support partner success.
- Stay updated on market trends and entrepreneurial developments within Spain to identify new opportunities.
- Organize and lead activities ranging from formal events to casual coffee chats to engage with the entrepreneurial community and drive market development.

Qualifications
Minimum Qualifications:
- Fluency in Spanish and English.
- Proven track record in business development or sales within the social media, tech, or entrepreneurial sectors.
- Strong network of entrepreneurs and/or agencies across Spain.
- Exceptional ability to identify and acquire high-potential partners.
- Results-driven with excellent analytical skills to inform decision-making under pressure.
- Outstanding communication and negotiation skills, with the ability to articulate complex concepts effectively.

Preferred Qualifications:
- 4+ years of relevant experience, preferably in live-streaming, video-sharing, social media, tech, or startups.
- Bachelor’s degree or higher in a relevant field.
- Experience creating and executing comprehensive business development strategies.
- Skilled in presenting tailored proposals that showcase the value of partnerships with TikTok LIVE.
- Self-motivated, proactive, and collaborative, thriving in cross-functional teams.
- Ability to adapt and work at a high pace.

About TikTok
TikTok is the leading destination for short-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.


Why Join Us
Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy - a mission we work towards every day.
We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.

Diversity & Inclusion
TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.

title: Growth Data Scientist
description:
Smadex is a leading advertising technology company founded in Barcelona in 2010 and sold to American-based and stock-listed Entravision in 2018 (NYSE::EVC). We are one of the top mobile ad-tech companies in the world and the largest Demand Side Platform (DSP) based in Europe, with our revenues over +$100M and consistently growing +40% YoY over the past years. And with an incredible 100% growth last year.

Our mission is to continue to improve our ad-tech platform, especially the core of it, our algorithms, to help our clients achieve their programmatic advertising campaign goals. We want to give our employees a job they’ll love, where they will be challenged to improve results through real-life engineering and data analysis and where everyone’s implication has an impact.

We are looking for a Growth Data Scientist to work closely with internal and external teams to help advertisers achieve their business goals and improve the effectiveness of Smadex Ads. The candidate should be communicative and analytical, in order to answer the complex questions that Product and Campaign performance are facing. For this purpose we use tons of data available to get powerful insights to resolve them, creating effective strategies to help improve the Smadex platform! 

This is a full-time position based in Barcelona.

Your tasks and responsibilities:

 * Dive deep into complex datasets, covering the behavior of millions of users and apps worldwide, to extract actionable learnings that inform high-level strategy and stakeholder understanding
 * Partner with business and technical teams to drive rigorous analysis and testing, identifying key opportunities to maximize the performance and effectiveness of Smadex campaigns.
 * Collaborate with stakeholders to channel market insights and performance data into our roadmap, directly influencing Product and ML strategies to enhance our platform's capabilities.

Skills and requirements:

 * +3 years of experience as a Data Analyst / Data Scientist
 * Bachelor's or Master's Degree in a Quantitative Field (Engineering, Statistics, Mathematics, Data Sciences,.)
 * Strong problem-solving skills, ability to formulate questions, run analysis to articulate a recommendation.
 * Proven working knowledge of Python, SQL and large data sets, understanding of analytic methodologies for data evaluation.
 * Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present analysis and conclusions.
 * Strong knowledge and experience of statistical concepts and hypothesis testing.
 * Knowledge of the ad-tech industry is a plus.

Please note that we do NOT provide VISA sponsorship. Candidates without a legal permit to work in Spain won't be considered.

What is in it for you:

 * Integrate a highly motivated and young team.
 * Great compensation package.
 * Top location at the heart of Barcelona in a penthouse office with a rooftop terrace, barbeque, and a fully stocked fridge.
 * Work from home: 2 days/week
 * Private Health Insurance
 * Access to LinkedIn Learning
 * Meal vouchers - Tickets Restaurant monthly allowance.
 * Monthly gym allowance: Choose between DiR Gym and Wellhub
 * Monthly TGIF events.
 * Regular team-building events.
 * Fun and friendly work environment with talented marketers and engineers from over 40+ countries
 * And more!

title: Marketing Assistant
description:
About Us

At VetPlus, we believe that every pet deserves the best possible care. That’s why, for over 30 years, we have been dedicated to developing high-quality veterinary nutraceuticals that support pet health and wellbeing. As part of the Tangerine Group, we are The Global Leader in veterinary nutraceuticals, delivering innovative solutions to vets in over 50 countries worldwide. Our success is built on strong relationships, exceptional service, and an ongoing commitment to excellence.

We’re now looking for a Marketing Assistant (MA) to join our Spanish subsidiary and help ensure our brand is consistently delivered to the highest standards.

About The Role

Reporting to the Head of Marketing, you will act as the key link between our UK Head Office and our Spanish team, ensuring all marketing outputs remain aligned.

In this varied, hands‑on role, you will translate and maintain marketing literature, support events and exhibitions, manage marketing inventory, and help deliver the local marketing plan. You will also spend time, approximately 8 days per month, visiting Vet practices across the country – working with them to strengthen the VetPlus presence in their practice.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone who is highly organised, detail‑focused, and ready to grow within a fast‑paced international marketing environment.

What You’ll Be Doing

Marketing Support


 * Work with the Head of Marketing to develop and execute the local marketing plan.
 * Translate and update all labels, packaging, product information, and marketing materials in line with SOPs and approval processes.
 * Manage all local marketing material inventory.
 * Update competitor analysis quarterly.
 * Support social media platforms with enquiries, content, and responses.
 * Participate in divisional Marketing meetings as required.
   
   

Sales Team Support


 * Administer the Lunch & Learn programme.
 * Maintain the Partnership and Loyalty programmes
 * Ensure all actions are recorded within the CRM system for ASM visibility.
   
   

Events & Conferences


 * Support the planning and delivery of exhibitions and conferences in collaboration with the Events Manager and Head of Marketing.
 * Ensure all events follow correct processes for booking, set‑up, accommodation and post‑event activity
   
   

Marketing Administration


 * Ensure all marketing materials receive appropriate sign‑off before printing.
 * Maintain up‑to‑date hard copies of all marketing literature.
 * Support annual Subsidiary Business Plan production
   
   

What We’re Looking For

Experience & Skills


 * At least 5 years’ experience in a similar marketing or administrative role.
 * Proven administrative skills in a customer‑driven environment
 * Fluent in written and spoken English.
 * Excellent communication skills and strong attention to detail
 * Confident using PowerPoint, Excel and Word
 * Ability to work independently with strong time‑management skills.
 * A full driving licence
   
   

Personal Qualities

We’re looking for someone who is:


 * A quick learner and self‑motivated.
 * Hard‑working, enthusiastic and able to multi‑task.
 * A confident communicator and team player.
 * Capable of delivering high standards and acting with integrity at all times.
 * Knowledge of the veterinary market or companion animals would be an advantage
   
   

Why Join Us?

You’ll be joining a supportive, ambitious organisation with a strong reputation for quality and innovation. You’ll work closely with international colleagues, gain exposure to a wide range of marketing activities, and contribute directly to the success of VetPlus in Spain.

We’re committed to helping our people grow and you’ll be encouraged to develop your skills and take on new challenges.

title: Mid-Market Account Executive (Spanish Speaking - Remote Europe)
description:
The AI orchestration of your wildest imagination.

n8n is the open workflow orchestration platform built for the new era of AI. We give technical teams the freedom of code with the speed of no-code, so they can automate faster, smarter, and without limits. Backed by a fiercely inventive community and 500+ builder-approved integrations, we’re changing the way people bring systems together and scale ideas for impact.

Since our founding in 2019, we’ve grown into a diverse team of over 160 - working across Europe and the US, connected by a shared builder spirit and with our centre of gravity in Berlin. Along the way, we’ve:


 * Cultivated a community of more than 650,000 active developers and builders
 * Earned 145k+ GitHub stars, making us one of the world’s Top 40 most popular projects
 * Been ranked as one of Europe’s most promising privately held SaaS startups (4th in Sifted’s 2025 B2B SaaS Rising 100)
 * Raised $240m to date, from Sequoia’s first German seed to our recent $180m Series C - bringing us to a $2.5bn valuation
 * And are grateful for our 94 eNPS score (most companies would call 70 excellent)
   
   

That’s the company we’ve built. Now we’d love to see what you can build. If you’re applying, try n8n out - whether you’re technical or not - and share a screenshot of your first workflow with us. The easiest place to start is here: app.n8n.cloud/register.

We’re in a defining moment of an incredible journey. Come and build with us.

We are looking for a driven Account Executive with a strong entrepreneurial mindset to reinforce our team of 6 AEs (3 mid-market) selling our Enterprise plans [https://n8n.io/pricing/?selfHosted=true]. You will be reporting to Maria, Senior Sales Manager.

Why this role is exciting:


 * Ownership: You’ll run the full sales cycle end-to-end (average 2–3 months), from discovery to negotiation and close. No hand-offs or silos, just clear ownership and impact.
 * Momentum: We’re seeing a sharp rise in demand from mid-sized technical teams who want more control over their automation infrastructure, the perfect ICP for our self-hosted Enterprise plan.
 * Manage and expand: This is a high-impact role where outbound drives growth. You’ll build strategic relationships and own deals end-to-end.
 * Strategic Impact: You’ll build relationships with decision-makers across DevOps, Engineering, and IT. These are often hands-on, technical buyers, looking for trusted advisors, not just sales reps.
 * Growth Environment: You’ll be at the heart of a startup scaling from Series A to B — where your feedback, creativity, and execution will directly shape how we build and sell.
 * Collaborative Culture: Work closely with Product, Solution Engineers, Marketing, and Customer Success, a cross-functional team that moves fast and shares success.
   
   

What you’ll be doing:


 * Own the entire sales process — from qualification to close — with average land deals of €35–45K ARR.
 * Test and scale outbound tactics to build a repeatable and sustainable pipeline.
 * Build long-term strategic relationships with engineering leaders and other technical buyers.
 * Drive sales efficiency by improving internal processes, content, and tooling.
 * Collaborate closely with Product to share customer feedback and influence roadmap decisions.
   
   

What we’re looking for:


 * 4+ years of sales experience at a BtoB SaaS company
 * Mid-Market experience: You’re used to closing 4 figure deals
 * High quota: You've held a +600K ARR quota in your current / previous roles
 * Top performer: You have a track record of being in the top 10% of your sales team
 * Highly tech-savvy: You’ve sold to highly technical stakeholders (IT operations, devops, secops, software engineers) and/or know how to code and/or have an engineering background.
 * Entrepreneurial mindset: You spot opportunities for growth, suggest a plan to address them, and execute it. You’re able to work independently without a ton of established structure or processes.
 * Hunter mindset: You have a proven track record in outbound sales.
 * Bonus points: You have experience with open source, developer tools, PLG companies and/or startups at Series A-C stage.
 * Language: Fluent in Spanish and English.
 * Location: Based in Europe.
   
   

n8n is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

We can sponsor visas to Germany; for any other country, you need to have existing right to work.

Our company language is English.

You care about diversity and inclusion? We do too! Check out our Diversity, Inclusion and Belonging initiatives at n8n (https://www.notion.so/n8n/Diversity-inclusion-and-belonging-n8n-c1bec2fff536422d868b1a438d990e35).

Location disclaimer: If you see multiple job postings for the same role, it is most likely because we're hiring remotely for this role and posting in different locations to make sure every potential candidate can see the role. Please apply to the location you're the most likely to work from in the future.

Benefits


 * Competitive compensation 💸 – We offer fair and attractive pay.
 * Ownership 💪 – Our core value is to “empower others,” and we mean it—you’ll get a slice of n8n with equity.
 * Work/life balance 🏖️ – We work hard but ensure you have time to recharge:
    * Europe: 30 days of vacation, plus public holidays wherever you are.
    * US: 15 vacation days, 8 sick days, plus public holidays wherever you are.

 * Health & wellness 🩺 –
    * Europe: We provide benefits according to local country norms.*
    * US: Multiple low-premium, low-deductible medical plans with coverage for individuals and families—plus a no-cost premium HDHP option with a pre-seeded HSA—along with dental and vision coverage.

 * Future planning 💰 –
    * Europe: We provide pension contributions according to local country norms.*
    * US: 401(k) retirement plan with a 4% employer match.

 * Financial security 🛡️ –
    * Europe: We provide benefits according to local country norms.*
    * US: Company-paid short-term and long-term disability insurance, plus life insurance to support you and your loved ones.

 * Career growth 📈 – We hire rising stars who grow with us! You’ll get €1K (or equivalent) per year to spend on courses, books, events, or coaching to level up your skills.
 * A passionate team 🤩 – We love our product, and we prove it with regular hackathons where we see who can build the coolest thing with it!

 * Giving back 🤝 – We're big fans of open source, and you'll get $100 per month to support projects you care about.
 * AI enablement 🤖 – We believe in working smarter—everyone gets an unlimited AI budget to explore and use the best tools to boost productivity and creativity.
 * Transparency 🙏 – We all know what everyone’s working on, how the company is doing—the whole shebang.
 * An ambitious but kind culture 😍 – People love working here—our eNPS for 2024 is 94!

title: Strategy Planner - Social
description:
About VML

VML is a leading creative company that combines brand experience, customer experience, and commerce, creating connected brands to drive growth. VML is celebrated for its innovative and human first, award-winning work for blue chip client partners including AstraZeneca, Colgate-Palmolive, Dell, Ford, Microsoft, Nestlé, The Coca-Cola Company, and Wendy's. The agency is recognized by the Forrester Wave™ Reports, as a Leader among Marketing Creative and Content Service Providers, Commerce Services, Global Digital Experience Services, Global Marketing Services and, most recently, Marketing Measurement & Optimization. In addition, VML’s specialist health network, VML Health, is one of the world’s largest and most awarded health agencies. VML’s global network is powered by 26,000 talented people across 55+ markets, with principal offices in Kansas City, New York, Detroit, London, São Paulo, Shanghai, Singapore, and Sydney.

VML is a WPP agency (NYSE: WPP). For more information, please visit  www.vml.com, and follow along on Instagram, LinkedIn, and X.

VML is a global creative company that builds genuine relationships between brands and people, putting technology at the service of creativity.

Our Strategic team is looking for a Strategy Planner to join us in Madrid.

We’re searching for someone with strong strategic thinking, a sharp cultural eye, and a genuine interest in social platforms, digital behaviors and what’s happening right now.

This role is ideal for a planner who thinks social-first, understands how ideas live and grow on platforms, and can also connect that thinking to broader omnichannel / 360º campaigns.

A key part of the role involves structuring strategic narratives, building decks and presenting strategy both internally and to clients.

Your role will include…


 * Researching culture, consumer behaviors, trends and emerging signals, with a strong focus on digital and social ecosystems.
 * Translating insights from research, platforms and culture into clear strategic opportunities.
 * Preparing benchmarks and competitor analysis, understanding how brands show up across online and offline touchpoints.
 * Writing creative briefs that clearly frame the strategic challenge and inspire strong ideas.
 * Defining and developing omnichannel strategic proposals, grounded in consumer truths and journeys, with social often acting as the starting point.
 * Structuring strategic narratives and building clear, compelling decks, both for internal alignment and client-facing presentations.
 * Presenting strategy internally and to clients, explaining thinking in a clear, confident and persuasive way.
 * Supporting the development of strategic presentations for local and international clients.
 * Tracking the effectiveness of strategies and helping optimize them when needed, working with brand and performance KPIs.
 * Managing a variety of projects (national, international, B2C, B2B).
 * Collaborating closely with different teams: Creativity, Social Media, Content, Data, Performance.
   
   

We’d love it if you have…


 * A degree in Advertising, Marketing or a related field. A postgraduate degree in Strategic Planning or Digital Strategy is a plus.
 * 3–4 years of experience as a Strategist / Planner in an agency, brand consultancy or marketing department.
 * Strong research skills, including desk research and familiarity with qualitative and quantitative methodologies.
 * A solid understanding of social platforms, formats and trends, and how brands can participate meaningfully in them.
 * Interest in digital culture, technology updates and platform evolution.
 * Strong presentation skills: ability to structure decks with a clear narrative, prioritize ideas and communicate strategy effectively.
 * Confidence presenting both internally and in front of clients, adapting tone and depth depending on the audience.
 * Languages: Spanish (native or fluent) and English (advanced / proficiency level – mandatory).
   
   

This role is for you if you…


 * Are naturally curious about culture, people, platforms and what’s new.
 * Enjoy connecting insights, creativity and business goals.
 * Have initiative, critical thinking and a proactive mindset.
 * Feel comfortable turning complex thinking into simple, clear stories.
 * Work well in collaborative, multidisciplinary teams.
 * Want to keep learning and growing as a strategist.
   
   

What we offer…


 * A collaborative, creative and open-minded environment.
 * The opportunity to work with leading local and international brands.
 * A flexible work model based on self-responsibility (3 days in the office / 2 days remote).
 * Training designed for both professional and personal development.
 * Flexible compensation options (restaurant card, transport card, nursery check).
 * No dress code, come as you are.
   
   

Ready to join us?

If you’ve made it this far, it’s probably because something resonated 😉

Apply to the offer and our People team will review your profile.

If there’s a match, we’ll reach out to get to know each other and tell you more about VML and the role.

Our process usually includes two or three interviews, and at some point, we may ask you to complete a small strategic challenge.

Are you in?

We believe the best work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach, with teams in the office an average of four days a week. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.

WPP (VML) is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers.

When you click "Submit Application", this will send any information you add below to VML. Before you do this, we think it's a good idea to read through our Recruitment Privacy Policy. California residents should read our California Recruitment Privacy Notice. This explains what we do with your personal data when you apply for a role with us, and, how you can update the information you have provided us with or how to remove it.

title: Assistant Station Manager
description:
Horticulture

Bachelor

Are you interested in an Assistant Station Manager Position; leading property and personnel on a farm operation located in Almería? Do you have experience in Vegetable production or the Seed Business? If so, apply for the full-time position in Almería.

The vacancy for an Assistant station Manager at Rijk Zwaan

Assistant station Manager is responsible for the cultivation and maintenance of crops and facilities. This implicates to lead the day-to-day activities of the station and rental locations: preparation of the greenhouse compartments, sowing, transplanting, nutrition, irrigation, crop protection, disease control, climate control, crop management, general maintenance of the farm and rotation of the crops. The position is also in charge of the maintenance of equipment and greenhouses. Furthermore, this position will be responsible for a big number of temporary workers as well as considerable number of fixed employees working in the farm. Thus, proven leadership of temporary and fixed employees as well as strong organizational skills are necessary.

The work is performed in collaboration with the Station Managers and the rest of farm team. Priority crops are tomato, melon, watermelon and experience with most of these crops is desired.

We are seeking a Station Manager with the following criteria:


 * Master’s degree in agriculture, Agronomy, Horticulture
 * Knowledge of seed business and plant genetics is desired
 * At least 2 years of experience managing a farm in agriculture, seed business, vegetable production, etc.
 * Ability to coordinate technically the crops
 * Strong interest in product knowledge
 * Strong interest in people development, Team oriented
 * Strong communication and leadership skills
 * Highly motivated, and able to work independently
 * Speak English fluently
   
   

What can you expect from us?

We like to invest in our professionals, and that means in your personal development and training!

As well as pleasant collaboration with colleagues in different departments and countries, we are pleased to offer you:


 * A permanent contract right from the start
 * Integration within a well performing team
 * Social Benefits
 * A range of training opportunities and courses
 * Scope to be enterprising and develop

title: Research Scientist/Data Analyst (Remote EU, B2B contract)
description:
About Mira

Mira is a San Francisco-based company specializing in hormonal health, providing integrative care and hormonal testing to over 200,000 customers. In 2023, they were recognized by Inc. 5000 as America's fastest-growing femtech company. We started our company to help women and individuals reach their parenthood dreams and make their fertility journey smoother.

Mira’s most important breakthrough was inventing the market's only FDA-compliant at-home fertility monitor with quantitative technology. Since the beginning, they have been on a mission to develop data-driven hormonal health solutions to help women make confident health decisions during every stage of their lives—from the menstrual stage to menopause. Mira offers solutions to test, boost, and navigate fertility—starting from comprehensive hormone testing and supplements to fertility coaching and online courses.

We are committed to helping our customers achieve the highest possible success rates and outcomes; that is why our focus is on personalized care, the use of the most cutting-edge technology, and science-backed data.

About The Position

We are looking for an experienced Research Scientist / Applied Data Scientist (Healthcare) to lead data-driven research that unlocks insights from large-scale health, public health, or consumer datasets.

This role is highly analytical and technically focused. You will independently formulate research questions, design and execute studies, analyze large datasets, and translate findings into publications and product insights. This is not a clinical trial role and does not require specialization in OB-GYN, IVD, or regulated clinical development. Instead, it is centered on data mining, statistical analysis, and real-world evidence generation in healthcare.

This is an industry role at a fast-moving startup. We are looking for someone who has worked in a company environment before, can move quickly, and owns projects end-to-end—from hypothesis generation to publication.

Requirements

Must-have:


 * Data analytics and programming skills (Python AND R)
 * PHD
 * Commercial experience
 * Background in healthcare
 * Experience in leading and executing a full study, from formulating the hypotheses to writing the paper
 * Scientific literature search ability
 * Experience in publishing a paper before
 * Experience with commercial product claims before and knowledge in extracting claims to maximize the business needs
 * Strong study/project management skills
 * Ability to talk to the collaborators, formulate, and manage the studies
 * Ability to perform independent data analytics to conclude studies
   
   

Nice To Have


 * Experience in the medical device industry before
 * Background in consumer health before
 * Commercial mindset
   
   

Responsibilities


 * Independently lead data-driven research studies end-to-end, including:
    * Formulating research questions and hypotheses
    * Designing study methodologies
    * Performing statistical and computational analyses on large datasets
    * Interpreting results and drawing actionable conclusions

 * Conduct large-scale data mining and exploratory analysis on healthcare, public health, or consumer datasets to uncover patterns, trends, and insights relevant to women’s health applications
 * Apply advanced analytical techniques to support:
    * Scientific validation of Mira’s products and algorithms
    * Discovery of new use cases and applications

 * Collaborate closely with data engineers, product managers, and algorithm teams to translate research findings into product improvements
 * Lead the writing and submission of peer-reviewed publications, white papers, generating claims for marketing, and technical reports
 * Present research findings clearly and logically to internal stakeholders, including leadership, product, and marketing teams
 * Ensure research is conducted ethically and in compliance with data privacy and governance standards
 * Contribute to the broader research strategy by identifying high-impact questions that can be answered through existing or newly acquired datasets




Benefits

What we offer


 * You will work with a dedicated, highly-engaged, international team of professionals who are passionate about helping couples and individuals start their families.
 * We have a fast-paced and collaborative work environment where we encourage open communication, ownership, and independence.
 * In addition to a competitive salary, we offer a performance-based bonus system based on OKR.
 * We provide professional development opportunities - training courses, workshops, and seminars.
   
   

Details

The role is remote, with a 40-hour workweek, flexible hours, and occasional overlap with the US team's/candidates' time zone.

Recruiting process

Step 1: ‘Screening call with HR’ - Step 2: Test task Step 3: ' Interview with CTO’ - Step 4: ‘Interview with the rest of the team’ Step 5: ‘Interview with CEO’

title: Product Controller
description:
Nuestro negocio se encuentra en plena expansión y buscamos el talento más apasionado, con energía positiva y capacidad de esfuerzo.


¿Quieres formar parte de un gran equipo humano, creativo y profesional? ¡Este es tu sitio!


En el equipo de DESIGN tenemos una vacante de PRODUCT CONTROLLER con el objetivo de asegurar la coherencia, viabilidad y correcta ejecución del desarrollo de las colecciones, actuando como figura transversal de coordinación, control y centralización de la información de producto, desde la planificación inicial hasta el lanzamiento por temporada, garantizando la alineación entre producto, pricing, data y los cronogramas de los procesos transversales en los que participa Diseño.


Entre sus funciones principales se encontrarían las siguientes:


Gestión y coordinación transversal de colecciones


   
   
 * Coordinar los procesos CROSS del desarrollo de colección y sus cronogramas (JIRA).
   
   
 * Centralizar y validar la información de producto a lo largo de todo el ciclo de vida de la colección.
   
   
 * Asegurar el cumplimiento de hitos, entregables y timmings interdepartamentales.
   
   


Merchandise Plan y estructura de colección


   
   
 * Coordinar el Merchandise Plan junto a Diseño, Purchasing y Dirección.
   
   
 * Analizar evoluciones de referencias, SKUs y estructuras de colección.
   
   
 * Preparar presentaciones de seguimiento y alineación para Dirección.
   
   


Pricing


   
   
 * Liderar la coordinación integral del proceso de pricing junto a Diseño, Purchasing y Dirección: definición de target; creación de calculadoras; seguimiento de márgenes, coordinación de rondas de negociación, cierre de tarifas.
   
   
 * Actuar como figura experta y de apoyo estratégico en pricing.
   
   


Metodología y foros de producto


   
   
 * Coordinar las reuniones de Metodología establecidas en la compañía.
   
   
 * Unificar, revisar y validar la documentación a presentar.
   
   
 * Coordinar cada sesión con las necesidades de producto y requerimientos establecidos a cada temporada.
   
   


Catálogo y muestras de catálogo


   
   
 * Gestionar el proceso completo de producción de catálogo: Pre-textos y textos de catálogo; Coordinación de muestras de catálogo con Marketing, Samples y Diseño; Revisión de catálogos: textos, fichas de producto y pruebas de color.
   
   
 * Centralizar actualizaciones y comunicación oficial de cambios de producto.
   
   
 * Coordinar disponibilidad y uso de muestras de catálogo entre áreas.
   
   
 * Coordinar las revisiones del catálogo por parte del equipo de Diseño en base al calendario de producción del catálogo.
   
   


Muestras de agente (SMS)


   
   
 * Gestionar el pedido de las muestras de agente (SMS) junto con Purchasing a cada temporada.
   
   
 * Gestionar el contenido de un muestrario junto con Customer Service y Samples a cada temporada.
   
   
 * Centralizar actualizaciones y comunicación oficial de cambios de producto en materia de muestrarios o muestras de agente.
   
   
 * Coordinar y asegurar la llegada y uso de las muestras de agente para convenciones.
   
   


Convención comercial


   
   
 * Preparar y validar contenido de producto para Convención a cada temporada.
   
   
 * Coordinar muestrarios, showroom y participación del equipo de Diseño en las jornadas de convención.
   
   
 * Recoger y trasladar feedback post-convención.
   
   


Comités y gobierno de producto


   
   
 * Actuar como secretaria del Product Committee.
   
   
 * Participar en foros estratégicos de producto donde participe Diseño (PASO Management Group).
   
   


Proyectos estratégicos y mejora continua


   
   
 * Liderar o co-liderar proyectos estratégicos del área (Maestro de Artículos, mejora de atributos, PLM y futuros)
   
   
 * Coordinar onboardings formativos en procesos CROSS a perfiles de Diseño u otros departamentos.
   
   
 * Contribuir con la aportación de resultados clave para cumplimiento de objetivos del área.
   
   


El puesto requiere una coordinación constante con distintas áreas de la compañía, manteniendo una visión transversal y global de los procesos, en colaboración con las áreas de Diseño, Purchasing, Packaging, Marketing, Commercial, Digital Business y Dirección.


¿Qué podemos ofrecerte?


* Flexibilidad horaria de entrada entre las 8h y las 9h, así como de comida y de salida ⏱


* Viernes de jornada intensiva


* Jornada intensiva durante los meses de julio y agosto ☀


* Política de Trabajo en Remoto del 20% de la jornada laboral semanal


* Eventos corporativos para que conozcas a todas las personas de YM


* Formación interna y online en habilidades, competencias e idiomas


* Metodología AGILE


* Descuento exclusivo en nuestro producto a través de un código de emplead@ YM


* Ambiente acogedor e instalaciones nuevas, con comedores y máquinas de vending, café y agua filtrada. Zonas de descanso ☕


* Equipo de trabajo con las mejores prestaciones


* Proceso de Onboarding en YM cuidando el mínimo detalle. Welcome pack para que no te preocupes por el desayuno y la bebida del primer día


* Parking interno de YM gratuito


* Descuento y servicio de recogida para el lavado de vehículo

title: Marketing Manager Restauración
description:
Desde la división de Sales & Marketing en WeHunt estoy buscando un Marketing Manager para una importante empresa del sector de la restauración organizada ubicada en Madrid, con más de 50 puntos de venta por toda España.

La principal función del puesto es redefinir y ejecutar la nueva estrategia de marketing de la compañía, asegurando el impacto en ventas a corto / medio plazo junto con los planes de expansión de la compañía.

 * Junto con Trade Marketing, garantizar coherencia en todos los puntos de contacto con el cliente.




Requisitos del perfil (H/M/D):

description:

¿Qué te ofrece Allianz?

Si buscas una oportunidad donde crecer, sentirte valorado/a y vivir con equilibrio, Allianz es para ti.

Además de acompañamiento gratuito durante el proceso de gestión del certificado y más horas de permiso, si eres tú el que tiene discapacidad y nos la acreditas recibirás 1500€ en tu primera nómina.

91061 | Venta y Distribución | Profesional / Senior | Allianz Spain | Jornada completa | Indefinido

El cuidado por nuestros/as empleados/as, sus ambiciones, sueños y desafíos es lo que nos hace un empleador único. Juntos/as, podemos crear un entorno donde todos/as se sientan empoderados/as y tengan la confianza para explorar, crecer y dar forma a un futuro mejor para nuestros/as clientes/as y el mundo que nos rodea.

En Allianz, creemos en la unidad: creemos que un mundo unido es un mundo más próspero y estamos comprometidos a apoyar constantemente oportunidades iguales para todos/as. La base de esto es nuestro lugar de trabajo inclusivo, donde las personas y el rendimiento importan y donde se cultiva una cultura basada en la integridad, la justicia, la inclusión y la confianza.

Por lo tanto, aceptamos solicitudes sin importar el origen étnico o cultural, la edad, el género, la nacionalidad, la religión, la clase social, la discapacidad o la orientación sexual, o cualquier otra característica protegida por las leyes y regulaciones locales vigentes.

Join us. Let's care for tomorrow.

title: Head of International Expansion - Retail Company
description:
Como Head of International Expansion liderarás el desarrollo de la compañía a nivel internacional, identificando mercados prioritarios y los modelos de entrada (vía puntos de venta propios, franquicias, master franquicias o joint ventures.

Detalles del cliente



Nuestro cliente es una compañía en pleno proceso de crecimiento y expansión internacional, con un modelo de negocio consolidado y una marca con fuerte potencial en mercados globales. Se trata de una organización dinámica, con una cultura orientada a la innovación, la excelencia operativa y la expansión sostenible a largo plazo. La empresa apuesta por el talento, la profesionalización de sus equipos y la expansión estructurada en nuevos países a través de modelos adaptados a cada mercado.




Descripción de la oferta

Bajo la dependencia del CEO de la compañía, el/la candidato/a se responsabilizará de:







Responsabilidades


 * Definir y liderar la estrategia de expansión internacional, identificando mercados clave y los modelos de entrada más adecuados (propio, franquicia, master franchise, JV, etc.).
 * Realizar estudios de mercado, análisis de competencia y evaluación de oportunidades en nuevos países.
 * Coordinar la apertura de nuevos mercados, garantizando la correcta implantación desde el punto de vista legal, operativo, financiero y comercial.
 * Negociar acuerdos estratégicos con partners locales, operadores, distribuidores o inversores.

 * Asegurar la correcta transferencia de procedimientos, estándares operativos y cultura corporativa a los equipos internacionales.
 * Coordinar el rendimiento de cada país, garantizando el cumplimiento de los estándares de calidad, marca y desempeño.
 * Analizar KPIs internacionales y definir acciones para mejorar ventas, rentabilidad y eficiencia.
 * Alinear a los equipos locales con los objetivos globales de la compañía.
 * Garantizar que la experiencia de la marca se exporta de manera consistente a cada mercado.
 * Colaborar con las áreas de Marketing y Producto para adaptar estrategias y propuestas cuando sea necesario.
 * Impulsar ajustes o innovaciones locales para adecuarse a la demanda de cada país sin comprometer la identidad de la marca.
 * Asegurar que todos los mercados cumplen los estándares operativos, de calidad, formación y supply chain.
 * Liderar auditorías de procesos y promover la mejora continua.
 * Impulsar la eficiencia operativa y el escalado sostenible en toda la red internacional.

 * Colaborar estrechamente con Operaciones, Marketing, Producto, Finanzas y RRHH para garantizar alineación global.
 * Reportar a Dirección General con visibilidad permanente sobre la evolución del proyecto internacional.
   



Perfil del candidato



Buscamos una persona con formación en ADE, Economía, Comercio Internacional, Marketing o áreas relacionadas, siendo valorable disponer de un MBA o posgrados en internacionalización o desarrollo de negocio global. Deberás aportar al menos 5 años de experiencia en expansión internacional en compañías de retail habiendo hecho desarrollo de franquicias, con trayectoria demostrada en apertura de mercados. Es esencial contar con visión estratégica, fuerte capacidad analítica, habilidades excelentes de negociación y gestión de partners, liderazgo inspirador, comunicación efectiva, orientación a resultados y capacidad de ejecución, además de alta adaptabilidad a entornos dinámicos y disponibilidad para viajar.
Se requiere castellano e inglés avanzados (C1/C2), valorándose otros idiomas como francés, alemán y portugués.







Oferta de empleo

Posibilidad de incorporarte a una compañía con un modelo de negocio más que validado, dónde podrás participar de manera activa en su desarrollo y crecimiento a nivel internacional.

Excelente paquete retributivo compuesto por salario fijo y variable.


Desired Skills and Experience

Buscamos a una persona con formación en ADE, Economía, Comercio Internacional, Marketing o áreas relacionadas, siendo valorable disponer de un MBA o posgrados en internacionalización o desarrollo de negocio global. Aportará una experiencia de al menos 5 años en una posición similar en compañías de retail habiendo hecho desarrollo de franquicias. title: Product Owner - Data Products
description:
Who are Yapily

Why we exist, and where we're headed:

Our Mission: Redefining how the world interacts with value.

Our Vision: A world without financial friction

Our Purpose: To empower everyone to access and move value

At Yapily, we're building a powerful, scalable, and secure open banking infrastructure that redefines how the world interacts with value.

Our open banking platform powers leading companies, such as Adyen, Intuit QuickBooks, and Google. By delivering payment initiation, bank data access, and pre-built products, we enable businesses to innovate fast and push the boundaries of financial technology.

As an early pioneer of open banking, we're actively shaping the future of this industry with unrivalled expertise and a relentless focus on innovation.

What we're looking for

We are looking for a Product Owner with strong financial data API products and API design expertise to own the execution and delivery of our Open Banking Data product across 19 UK & European markets. You will manage the backlog for data product engineering teams, working hands-on with Data products (API, Hosted or SDKs), bank and country expansions, consent management, and account information services.

This is a highly technical, execution-focused role where you translate product strategy into delivered features.  You are self-sufficient in analysing bank API documentation, testing data flows with Postman, and writing detailed technical requirements. You leverage AI tools to enhance productivity in requirements analysis, documentation, and testing workflows. You work closely with our Product Managers and collaborate with our Analyst and Operations teams to deliver world-class data connectivity capabilities. 

Learn more: https://www.yapily.com/company/about-us

Requirements

Key Tasks ...


 * Own and execute on the product backlog for engineering teams focused on open banking data products (AIS) across UK & European markets, translating product strategy from Product Managers into deliverable features
 * Lead sprint planning, daily standups, and delivery of data features with clear user stories and acceptance criteria
 * Conduct hands-on technical analysis of bank API documentation for account information endpoints
 * Test data APIs using tools like Postman to understand response schemas, data structures, and edge cases
 * Understand and document account structures: account identifier formats, transaction data, balance types across different countries
 * Analyse transaction data schemas and variations between banks and countries
 * Review consent management flows and data privacy requirements per jurisdiction
 * Collaborate with Business Analyst and Bank Operations team for market intelligence and institution testing
 * Coordinate with other PMs and POs for cross functional initiatives
 * Make rapid prioritisation decisions during sprints to maintain delivery velocity
 * Provide input to product strategy based on technical feasibility and implementation learnings
   
   

This list is not exhaustive, and there may be other tasks that we ask you to be involved in from time to time. Above all, you are flexible and want to add value wherever you can. You understand that in a start-up, priorities change and we all need to be adaptable. Not only will you be comfortable when asked to input and lead in other areas, but you'll be comfortable being a self-starter and instigating changes yourself.

For your new role


 * You have 4-6 years of experience as a Product Owner, Product Manager, Business Analyst, or Technical Product role in fintech or financial services
 * You have understanding of financial data: account information, transaction data, balances, and financial data structures
 * You are highly technical: you can read API documentation, understand REST APIs, and test with tools like Postman
 * You have experience with Open Banking, account aggregation, or financial data connectivity beyond Open Banking
 * You have proven experience owning product backlogs and leading agile delivery
 * You can write clear user stories, acceptance criteria, and technical specifications
 * You are self-sufficient in conducting technical analysis while knowing when to leverage specialist support
 * You have understanding of data privacy, consent management, and GDPR/PSD2 requirements
 * You have excellent communication skills in English
 * You have strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to data quality
 * You are eager to learn and grow toward a Product Manager role
 * You are driven and curious. You ask questions and strive to understand
 * You are comfortable working in a fast-paced, evolving environment with ambiguity
 * You think outside the box and are pragmatic
 * You have a can-do approach and switching up tasks comes naturally to you
   
   

Benefits


 * 25 days holiday a year (plus bank holidays) in the UK, 20 days in Lithuania, 23 days in Spain, 22 days in Germany
 * We also offer an additional holiday day after 1 year's service, up to the value of 5 days
 * We're serious about work/life balance and operate hybrid working, giving you the flexibility to work from home, working from our amazing office space or work abroad for up to 30 days per year ‘Nomad Working'
 * Comprehensive Private Medical Insurance
 * Life Assurance and Income Protection (UK only)
 * Company Pension Scheme
 * Enhanced Paternity and Maternity leave
 * £200 annual Learning and Personal Development budget
 * Cycle To Work Scheme
 * Employee Referral Scheme which offers £1,000 per employee referral
 * Monthly social budget
 * Daily snacks provided in the office
 * Dog friendly office
   
   

OUR VALUES

We obsess about quality


 * Our customers have entrusted us with a critical function in a regulated industry...and we take that responsibility seriously. We always assume ownership and hold ourselves accountable
   
   

We are curious


 * Our innovation is powered by our collective growth mindset. We're lifelong learners who challenge assumptions, experiment, and iterate
   
   

We act with integrity


 * We're guided by our mission and earn and maintain trust by doing what's right, even when it's not easy
   
   

We are do-ers


 * We reject indifference and agility is our strength.
 * We're motivated by challenges, and biassed towards action
   
   

We problem-solve together


 * We're diverse people in diverse places, and know the best solutions are born out of collaboration. We win, lose, and learn...together

title: Portfolio Operations Manager
description:
Keywords Studios provides creative services to the global video games industry and beyond through an end-to-end platform, supercharged by its own technology. Keywords Studios' goal is to help clients imagine more by partnering in bringing to life digital content that entertains, connects, and educates people worldwide.

The Globalize Operations Manager leads strategic initiatives that modernize and optimize Globalize operations, ensuring alignment with the organization's people strategy and overall business objectives. This role oversees cross-functional programs focused on process improvement, digital enablement, organizational effectiveness, and change management.

About The Role

We are looking for a Portfolio Operations Manager to ensure that portfolio initiatives move forward efficiently.

In this role, you will be responsible for driving execution across a portfolio of initiatives, ensuring alignment with strategic priorities defined by the Globalize Portfolio Director. While strategy and direction are set at the leadership level, you own coordination, momentum, and delivery.

This is a highly execution-focused role, ideal for someone who thrives in complex, multi-stakeholder environments and knows how to move work forward without formal authority.

Key Responsibilities


 * Track and manage portfolio initiatives using an OKR-based framework
 * Coordinate cross-functional teams to ensure alignment and execution
 * Prepare clear, concise, executive-level progress updates
 * Identify risks, dependencies, and blockers early
 * Push for decisions when ambiguity or misalignment slows progress
 * Adapt, pause, or close initiatives that no longer make strategic or operational sense

title: Finance Manager, Group Accounting Southern Europe
description:
About Permira

Permira is a global investment firm that backs successful businesses with growth ambitions. Founded in 1985, the firm advises funds across two core asset classes, private equity and credit, with total committed capital of approximately €80bn. The Permira private equity funds make both long-term majority (Buyout) and minority (Growth Equity) investments in four key sectors: Technology, Consumer, Healthcare, Services. The Permira credit funds support businesses with flexible financing solutions across Direct Lending, Strategic Opportunities and CLO Management. Established in 2007, the firm advises investment funds and products which have provided approximately €20 billion of debt capital to over 300 businesses.

Role Overview

Permira is searching for a professional, ambitious and driven Finance Manager to join the Spanish Office. Reporting to the Head of Group Accounting in the UK within the Firm Finance function, the primary day to day responsibilities will include core financial accounting functions and coordination of the south Europe cluster. This role will have oversight & ownership of the financial statements & will perform the closing processes and reconciliations, cash management, review and preparation of financial statements and periodic financial reporting.

Permira’s Group Accounting team is based predominately across London, Guernsey & Madrid, but with members also in New York, Singapore, and Hong Kong, and a growing number of our other European offices. The Group Accounting team totals 26 FTE and the overall Finance function (including Treasury, Transformation and Financial Planning & Reporting) is in excess of 40 FTE. The role will require collaboration both throughout the broader Finance function but also with other functional departments (ie Operations, Tax, HR, IT, Legal, Compliance).

Key Responsabilities

Financial Accounting & Control:


 * Overall responsibility for the financial management of the Madrid and Milan offices.
 * Oversight and review of bookkeeping activities performed by the 3rd party provider (Infor Sun), including ownership of monthly balance sheet reconciliations and control framework.
 * Approval and execution of bank payments, ensuring compliance with internal controls and segregation of duties.
 * Ownership of data upload and reconciliation into the Group consolidation system (OneStream), ensuring accuracy and alignment with Group reporting standards.
 * Preparation and presentation of periodic management reporting packs, including variance analysis and commentary to senior stakeholders.
 * Full ownership of the annual budgeting and forecasting process for the Madrid and Milan offices, including stakeholder engagement and cost control monitoring.
 * Management of the Abort Cost process, ensuring appropriate allocation, tracking and reporting.
 * Oversight and approval of staff expenses and management of the corporate Amex programme, including policy enforcement and compliance monitoring.
 * Continuous improvement of finance processes, systems and internal controls.
   
   

Audit/Statutory


 * Lead and coordinate annual audit processes for Southern European entities, ensuring delivery within agreed timetables.
 * Primary liaison with Group and local auditors.
 * Responsibility for preparation and review of statutory financial statements under Spanish and Italian GAAP.
 * Organisation and coordination of local board meetings, including preparation of supporting financial documentation.
 * Ensure compliance with local regulatory and corporate governance requirements.
   
   

Payroll Administration


 * Coordination and review of payroll processes in collaboration with external payroll providers.
 * Oversight of employee onboarding and offboarding processes from a finance and administrative perspective.
 * Financial management of employee benefits administration (Medical/Life insurance, Fudeat, flexible retribution), holiday and sickness records.
 * Liaison with HR, Legal and external advisors on employment-related financial matters.
   
   

Other


 * Act as key finance contact for Southern Europe, partnering with Operations, Tax, Legal, Compliance and HR.
 * Management of relationships with external auditors, administrators, payroll providers, banks and other service providers.
 * Support cross-border initiatives within the Firm’s global finance function.
 * Regular travel within the Firm’s network (principally Milan, Paris and London).
 * Contribution to global finance projects and professional events.
 * Scope for further regional or international responsibility within the Firm.
   
   

Desired Skills & Characteristics

We are looking for someone with a strong work ethic, who can work as part of a close team, but also be a self-starter. The successful candidate should be professional, demonstrate excellent communication and inter-personal skills, and have the desire and ambition to develop a career within a leading global Private Equity Firm.


 * Desired experience:
    * Qualified accountant (or equivalent) with 5-9 years of experience
    * Strong experience finance manager for a small to medium sized office
    * Sound understanding of financial accounting and reporting processes
    * Experience of working for an international organisation
    * Experience of working with major ERP systems and reporting tools would be useful but not essential (Onestream and Infor Sun). High level excel skills.
    * Excellent project management skills.
      
      

 * Key attributes
    * Excellent organisational skills. The role requires the individual to be able to manage multiple tasks simultaneously across a variety of products and work streams
    * Demonstrable ability to maintain the highest level of confidentiality at all times
    * Highly personable and a natural collaborator, with the desire and ability to create relationships across the Group’s network
    * Strong communication skills and fluent English and Spanish, both written and verbal.
    * Demonstrable experience working in a matrix, ambiguous environment.
    * Proactive approach with the ability to take initiative and apply sound judgement
    * Highly driven and motivated individual, with the patience and ambition to build a rewarding career within private equity.
      

To learn how we handle your personal data during the recruitment process, please review our Candidate Privacy Policy. This includes our Supplemental CCPA Notice of Collection where applicable.

title: Supply Chain Manager
description:
About Prometeon Tyre Group

Prometeon Tyre Group, fundado en 2017 como spin‑off de Pirelli, es el único fabricante global de neumáticos totalmente especializado en los segmentos Industrial, Agro y OTR.

Con más de 8.000 empleados, 4 plantas de producción, 4 centros de I+D y operaciones en más de 160 países, Prometeon desarrolla soluciones de neumáticos de alto rendimiento y sostenibles para el transporte de mercancías, personas y aplicaciones heavy‑duty.

Nuestro entorno multicultural, junto con una fuerte cultura técnica e industrial, nos posiciona como socio de referencia para operadores de flotas, distribuidores y clientes industriales a nivel global.




Supply Chain Manager Iberia

El/La Supply Chain Manager Iberia es responsable de la gestión integral de la cadena de suministro en España y Portugal, asegurando la disponibilidad óptima de producto, la eficiencia logística y el control de costes, en alineación con los objetivos comerciales del clúster Iberia.

El rol actúa como punto de conexión entre Ventas, Finanzas, Operaciones Europeas, HQ y proveedores logísticos, garantizando un flujo de suministro fiable, eficiente y escalable en un entorno multinacional.




Responsabilidades principales

 * Asegurar la disponibilidad de producto para cumplir los objetivos comerciales mensuales, gestionando demanda, inventarios, stock y asignaciones por canal.
 * Planificar y gestionar órdenes MRP, transferencias entre plantas, centros logísticos y operadores externos.
 * Coordinar operaciones logísticas end‑to‑end, incluyendo entregas directas a clientes y gestión de incidencias.
 * Gestionar proveedores logísticos y 3PL, asegurando SLA, niveles de servicio, tiempos de tránsito y mejora continua.
 * Monitorizar y analizar KPIs de supply chain y costes logísticos, elaborando reportes para management y HQ.
 * Gestionar master data y procesos SAP con impacto en planificación y logística.
 * Participar en proyectos internacionales de Supply Chain y en iniciativas regionales West Europe.




Perfil requerido

 * Grado o Máster en Ingeniería, Supply Chain, Logística, ADE o similar.
 * Mínimo 8 años de experiencia en supply chain, logística, planificación u operaciones en entornos complejos o industriales.
 * Experiencia sólida en SAP, planificación de demanda e inventarios y herramientas de análisis de datos.
 * Inglés fluido; español fluido o nativo; italiano muy valorado.
 * Perfil analítico, orientado a resultados, con capacidad de liderazgo transversal y gestión de stakeholders.
 * Experiencia en entornos multinacionales y disponibilidad para viajes ocasionales en Europa.




¿Por qué unirte a nosotros?

Porque tendrás un rol clave en la supply chain Iberia, con impacto directo en el negocio y exposición internacional, dentro de un grupo industrial solido y en crecimiento.

title: RESPONSABLE DE LOGÍSTICA INTERNA Y FLUJOS OPERATIVOS
description:
En estos momentos, como consultora de reclutamiento, estamos buscando para nuestro cliente un/a Responsable de logística interna y flujos operativos para una empresa industrial, con proyección internacional, que apuesta por la innovación, la sostenibilidad y la excelencia operativa. Buscamos incorporar una figura clave que lidere y optimice los flujos logísticos internos y los procesos operativos, con impacto real en el negocio.

FUNCIONES Y TAREAS


 * Organización y mejora de los flujos internos de materiales, semielaborados y producto terminado.
 * Análisis y rediseño de circuitos logísticos, layouts y recorridos internos.
 * Optimización de movimientos, reducción de tiempos muertos, reprocesos y manipulaciones innecesarias.
 * Implantación de estándares operativos y mejora de la eficiencia global.
 * Gestión integral de almacenes y stocks: organización física y lógica, control de inventarios, rotaciones y obsolescencias.
 * Definición de sistemas de ubicación, picking y metodologías FIFO/FEFO.
 * Coordinación con producción, compras y área comercial para asegurar el suministro y evitar cuellos de botella.
 * Preparación y expedición de pedidos con foco en fiabilidad y nivel de servicio.
 * Uso e implantación de herramientas de control y digitalización (ERP, Excel avanzado, BI).
 * Definición y seguimiento de KPIs logísticos y cuadros de mando operativos.
 * Aplicación de metodologías de mejora continua (Lean, 5S, Kaizen, SMED si aplica).
 * Liderazgo de pequeños proyectos de optimización.
 * Organización, coordinación y formación del equipo operativo, cuando aplique.
   
   

REQUISITOS


 * Formación en Ingeniería Industrial, Organización Industrial, Logística, ADE o similar.
 * Experiencia de entre 3 y 7 años en entornos industriales u operativos.
 * Experiencia demostrable en mejora de procesos, no solo en gestión operativa.
 * Conocimientos sólidos en gestión de almacenes, control de inventarios, flujos de materiales y procesos productivos.
 * Manejo avanzado de Excel, ERP y herramientas de control.
 * Perfil analítico, estructurado y orientado a la eficiencia.
 * Capacidad para trabajar a pie de planta, con enfoque práctico y resolutivo.
 * Autonomía, criterio propio y capacidad de coordinación transversal.
 * Inglés nivel B2 o superior, con capacidad para comunicarse con proveedores internacionales.
 * Valorable formación en Lean, Supply Chain o mejora de procesos.
   
   

¿Qué ofrece la empresa?


 * Incorporación a una empresa sólida y en crecimiento, con visión internacional.
 * Salario 40.000€ SBA
 * Puesto estratégico, con autonomía y capacidad real de impacto en la organización.
 * Entorno que apuesta por la innovación, la mejora continua, la sostenibilidad y el liderazgo humano.
 * Desarrollo profesional a largo plazo.
 * Horario flexible: de lunes a jueves de 8:30 a 18:00 y viernes de 8:30 a 14:30.
 * Contrato indefinido a tiempo completo.

title: EU Training Manager
description:
🏢 About the company

OMODA Motors, part of the Chery Group, is an international automotive brand expanding in Europe. Our European headquarters is in Barcelona, leading the brand’s development across the region.




📍 Work arrangement

This position is based fully on-site (no remote) at our EU HQ office (Cornella, Barcelona).




💡 Why join us?

• Join the European leadership team of a fast-growing global automotive brand.

• Drive sales enablement strategies across EU subsidiaries and dealer networks.

• Play a strategic role in building a high-performance training organization in the European market.

• Work closely with global, regional, and local stakeholders.

• Contribute directly to sustainable sales growth and dealer capability enhancement.

• Long-term leadership development opportunities within a global automotive group.




🔧 Key responsibilities

• Take overall responsibility for training and enablement across EU subsidiaries, building a professional and effective training organization to support sustainable sales growth.

• Lead, develop, and empower training managers in EU subsidiaries by defining capability standards, training plans, assessment criteria, and coaching mechanisms.

• Ensure subsidiary training managers effectively fulfill their core mission of supporting dealers to improve sales performance.

• Establish and maintain regular communication and collaboration with EU general distributors and key market partners on training-related initiatives.

• Work with partners to design and implement incentive mechanisms that enhance engagement and execution of training enablement programs.

• Closely collaborate with EU and subsidiary sales leaders to identify key sales drivers and performance gaps through data analysis, market research, and frontline insights.

• Integrate resources from Global HQ, Regional HQ, country organizations, and distributors to design and execute annual and quarterly training enablement plans aligned with sales targets.

• Conduct on-site coaching and support for key dealers, addressing practical sales challenges and accelerating performance improvement.

• Systematically capture best practices and successful cases from high-performing dealers, standardize and replicate these experiences across the EU region.

• Support regional initiatives and special projects, including organizing study programs and exchange visits to Global HQ for outstanding trainers and sales consultants.

• Complete other training enablement-related tasks assigned by the EU regional management team.




✅ Requirements

• Bachelor’s degree or above; majors in Marketing, Business Administration, Human Resources (training focus), or related fields are preferred.

• Minimum 5 years of experience in training management, with at least 3 years of training enablement experience in the automotive, retail, or related industries within the European market.

• Proven experience in team management and cross-functional and cross-partner collaboration.

• Demonstrated success in driving sales performance improvement through structured training enablement initiatives.

• Strong business insight with a deep understanding of EU market characteristics, customer behavior, and sales logic.

• Excellent resource integration and stakeholder coordination capabilities across global, regional, and local levels.

• Fluent in English; proficiency in additional major EU languages (e.g. Spanish, German, French) is a strong plus.

• Strong program design, execution, and performance tracking skills.

• Results-oriented, resilient under pressure, adaptable to change, with strong learning agility and innovation mindset.




🎁 What we offer

• Leadership opportunity within a global automotive brand expanding across Europe.

• Strategic role directly impacting EU sales performance.

• Multicultural working environment.

• Long-term career development opportunities.

• Salary: Negotiable.

title: PEOPLE ANALYTICS & HRIS, EXPERT
description:
En Mango vestimos de pasión todo lo que hacemos. Con origen en Barcelona y presencia en más de 120 países, inspiramos al mundo con creatividad, innovación y autenticidad.

Nuestro equipo multicultural es el motor de nuestro éxito. Nos enorgullece llevar la moda más allá, conectando nuestro estilo único con personas de todo el mundo.

TUS PRINCIPALES RESPONSABILIDADES:

SOBRE TI:


 * Experiencia demostrada en la aplicación e integración de análisis avanzados y predictivos, métricas de personal e información para el desarrollo de resultados estratégicos y operativos.
 * Experiencia previa en la evaluación de procesos de contratación en una organización, identificando requisitos y soluciones para cumplir con los objetivos de personal.
 * Experiencia en la toma de decisiones estratégicas relacionadas con el personal, como contrataciones, formación y desarrollo, gestión del talento, diversidad y cumplimiento de recursos humanos.
 * Formación superior en un campo relevante, como Recursos Humanos, Psicología Organizacional, Estadística o un campo relacionado.
 * Experiencia en un puesto de liderazgo, preferiblemente como Team Leader, con la capacidad de coordinar y facilitar el trabajo de otros, y gestionar equipos de profesionales junior y con experiencia moderada.
 * Valorable experiencia en Workday y/o SuccessFactors.

TUS BENEFICIOS:


 * Disfruta de un horario flexible y trabajo híbrido que se adapta a tus necesidades. En Mango, apostamos por la conciliación de la vida profesional y personal.
 * En Mango, el fin de semana empieza el viernes a mediodía. Ofrecemos horario intensivo los viernes y vísperas de festivos.
 * Planifica tus vacaciones según tus preferencias. En Mango, valoramos tu tiempo y queremos que disfrutes de cada momento.
 * Como parte del equipo de Mango, tendrás descuento en todas nuestras líneas, ¡para que estés siempre a la última!
 * Paquete de retribución flexible con ventajas fiscales: seguro médico, formación, catering y programa de guardería.
 * Transporte de empresa gratuito desde Barcelona y El Vallés.
 * En Mango, invertimos en tu crecimiento personal y profesional. Accede a una oferta de formación variada, mentorías personalizadas, programas de desarrollo continuo y oportunidades de promoción interna que te impulsarán hacia el éxito. A nivel técnico, podrás formarte en el uso de diferentes plataformas tecnológicas, así como participar en workshops, meetups, comunidades de prácticas, team buildings y company meetings.
 * ¡Piensa en grande! Mango te ofrece oportunidades internacionales en más de 120 mercados para expandir tu horizonte y crecer junto con nosotros a nivel global.
   
   
   

En Mango, creemos en una cultura inclusiva donde la creatividad y la innovación nos inspiran a llevar la moda más allá. Por ello, tenemos el compromiso de ofrecer igualdad de oportunidades para todas las personas, valorando la autenticidad de cada individuo.
